text: The Hebrew text has ‘between the evenings’, it is not clear in what sense.

‘The Pasch’; the Vulgate here, and in the Old Testament generally, transliterates the Hebrew name of this feast as ‘Phase’. The alternative form ‘pasch’ is given here as being more familiar to the modern reader, and universal in the Vulgate rendering of the New Testament.
‘The powers’; literally, ‘the gods’, but it seems possible that human powers are meant, cf. note on Ex. 21.6 below.
‘Lost to Israel’; cf. note on Gen. 17.14.
‘Or break it up into joints’; literally, ‘no bone of it shall be broken’; cf. Jn. 19.36.
